title: What’s the Counteroffer Acceptance Rate?
bodyHtml:
The Counteroffer Acceptance rate is an important indicator of how satisfied your Back Market customers are with the quality of your Trade-in order management.

The Counteroffer Acceptance Rate represents the number of counteroffers that are accepted by Back Market customers versus all counteroffers. It’s calculated on a 30-day basis.
 

Why is it important to have a high Counteroffer Acceptance Rate?

If a Back Market customer rejects your counteroffer, it’s bad for both you and the customer. 

The Back Market customer trusted Back Market enough to trade their item in, put in a lot of effort to assess and send the item to you, and in the end, never got paid. And they won’t be able to resell their item somewhere else until you return it to them. Less than 1% of Back Market customers are satisfied after rejecting a counteroffer.

From your perspective, you spent a lot of time processing the order, only to be unable to repair and resell the item.

It's in everyone's interest to avoid ending up in this situation.
 

How to keep your Counteroffer Acceptance Rate high

Please read the “How to make a counteroffer?” section in this article carefully.

 

How to monitor your performance indicators

You can monitor your Counteroffer Acceptance Rate in your Trade-in quality dashboard accessible from the sales part of your Back Office.

Each of your Trade-in performance indicators has a limit. Check out this article on monitoring periods to learn more. If you enter a monitoring period, we'll let you know by email and provide you with the next steps.

If you have any questions, reach out to the Seller Support Center or your Back Market point of contact.
